title: SAT Reading & Writing
description: Reading comprehension, grammar, and effective expression.

questions.passage: The octopus is among the most intelligent invertebrates. In laboratory settings, octopuses have learned to open jars, navigate mazes, and even recognize individual human handlers — feats once thought to require a backboned brain.
questions.prompt: The main purpose of the passage is to:

choices.text: highlight the surprising intelligence of octopuses
argue that octopuses should not be kept in labs

questions.explanation: The passage lists cognitive feats to emphasize that octopuses are surprisingly intelligent for invertebrates.

questions.prompt: Choose the option that makes the sentence grammatically correct: "Each of the students ______ responsible for their own project."
questions.explanation: "Each" is singular, so it takes the singular verb "is."

questions.prompt: Which choice best combines the sentences? "The experiment failed. The researchers learned a great deal from it."

choices.text: The experiment failed, the researchers learned a great deal from it.
The experiment failed, but the researchers learned a great deal from it.
The experiment failed the researchers learned a great deal from it.
The experiment failed; and the researchers learned a great deal from it.

questions.explanation: A comma plus the coordinating conjunction "but" correctly joins two independent clauses and signals contrast. Option A is a comma splice.

choices.text: Due to the fact that it was raining, we stayed inside.
Because it was raining, we stayed inside.
On account of the rain that was falling, we stayed inside.
It was raining, and for that reason we stayed inside.

questions.explanation: "Because" conveys the same cause-and-effect meaning most concisely.
